title: Andhra Pradesh Heatwave Alert: Temperatures to Reach 44°C in Multiple Districts - Good Morning Andhra
full_text:
A severe Andhra Pradesh Heatwave alert has been issued as temperatures continue to soar across the state. According to officials, intense heat conditions are gripping multiple districts, with mercury levels already crossing 40°C in hundreds of mandals. Authorities have urged citizens to remain cautious as the situation is expected to worsen over the next two days.

Andhra Pradesh Heatwave Intensifies Across Districts
The Andhra Pradesh Heatwave is expected to peak on April 16, with districts like Nandyal, Palnadu, Kadapa, Kurnool, Nellore, Eluru, and West Godavari likely to witness maximum temperatures between 43°C and 44°C.
Prakhar Jain, Managing Director of the Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority, stated that over 275 mandals have already recorded temperatures above 40°C. He further warned that around 34 mandals may face severe heatwave conditions, while 67 mandals could experience heatwave impact.
The Andhra Pradesh Heatwave is not expected to subside immediately, with similar conditions likely to persist on Friday as well.

Highest Temperatures Recorded
Several regions have recorded alarming temperatures under the ongoing Andhra Pradesh Heatwave:
These extreme readings indicate the severity of the Andhra Pradesh Heatwave, raising serious concerns for public health and safety.

Health Advisory and Precautions
Officials have issued strong advisories urging people to take precautions during the Andhra Pradesh Heatwave. Residents are advised to avoid stepping out during peak afternoon hours and to stay hydrated.

title: Heatwave Intensifies In AP, 275 Mandals Cross 40°C - Dailyhunt
full_text:
Visakhapatnam: Severe heat conditions continued across Andhra Pradesh on Wednesday, disrupting normal life, with temperatures crossing 40°C in 275 mandals, APSDMA Managing Director Prakhar Jain said.
Temperatures are expected to touch 43-44°C in districts including Parvathipuram Manyam, West Godavari, Eluru, NTR, Palnadu, Markapuram, Nellore, Kurnool, Nandyal and Kadapa. As many as 34 mandals are likely to face severe heatwave conditions, while 67 mandals may experience heatwaves across multiple districts.
On Friday, heatwave conditions are expected in 28 mandals (severe) and 37 mandals (moderate).
Sanjamala in Nandyal recorded the highest temperature at 44.6°C, followed by Gurazala (44.5°C), Kadapa (44.1°C), Anumalapalli (44°C) and Laddagiri (43.9°C).
Authorities have advised people to take precautions as temperatures continue to rise.
